Package org.apache.juneau.commons.settings

Class PropertyLookupResult

java.lang.Object
org.apache.juneau.commons.settings.PropertyLookupResult
public final class PropertyLookupResult extends Object
Tri-state result of a property lookup.

This type disambiguates source lookup semantics:

  • missing() - The key does not exist in the source.
  • present(Optional) with Optional.empty() - The key exists and resolves to a null value.
  • present(Optional) with a value - The key exists and resolves to a non-null value.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    boolean
    isPresent()
    Returns true if the key is present in this source.
    static PropertyLookupResult
    missing()
    Returns a result indicating the key is absent in this source.
    static PropertyLookupResult
    present(String value)
    Returns a result indicating the key is present with the specified value.
    static PropertyLookupResult
    present(Optional<String> value)
    Returns a result indicating the key is present with the specified value.
    Optional<String>
    value()
    Returns the resolved value for present results.

  • Method Details

    • missing

      public static PropertyLookupResult missing()
      Returns a result indicating the key is absent in this source.
      Returns:
      A missing result.

    • present

      public static PropertyLookupResult present(Optional<String> value)
      Returns a result indicating the key is present with the specified value.
      Parameters:
      value - The value optional. Must not be null.
      Returns:
      A present result.

    • present

      public static PropertyLookupResult present(String value)
      Returns a result indicating the key is present with the specified value.
      Parameters:
      value - The value.
      Returns:
      A present result.

    • isPresent

      public boolean isPresent()
      Returns true if the key is present in this source.
      Returns:
      true if present.

    • value

      public Optional<String> value()
      Returns the resolved value for present results.
      Returns:
      The value, never null.
